requirements.title: Timestamp Representation
requirements.description: The time module shall represent time as a struct with integer seconds and fixed-point fractional subseconds fields.
requirements.rationale: Integer seconds with fixed-point subseconds provides deterministic, lossless time representation without floating-point rounding, which is essential for real-time scheduling and time-triggered architectures.

requirements.title: Time API Interface
requirements.description: The time module shall define a vtable-based API with Now, AddTime, SubtractTime, SleepTo, Sleep, and conversion operations.
requirements.rationale: A vtable-based time API decouples the scheduler and application logic from platform-specific clock hardware, enabling both real hardware clocks and deterministic test clocks.

requirements.title: AddTime Operation
requirements.description: The time module shall add a duration to a timestamp in-place.
requirements.rationale: In-place addition avoids allocating a new timestamp, and is the fundamental operation for computing deadlines and advancing time.

requirements.title: AddTime Subsecond Carry-Over
requirements.description: The time module shall carry over subseconds to seconds when subsecond addition overflows.
requirements.rationale: Correct carry-over from subseconds to seconds is essential for accurate time arithmetic; without it, subsecond accumulation would overflow and produce incorrect timestamps.

requirements.title: SubtractTime Operation
requirements.description: The time module shall subtract a duration from a timestamp in-place.
requirements.rationale: In-place subtraction is the fundamental operation for computing elapsed time and remaining durations.

requirements.title: SubtractTime Subsecond Borrow
requirements.description: The time module shall borrow from seconds when subsecond subtraction underflows.
requirements.rationale: Correct borrow from seconds to subseconds is essential for accurate time subtraction; without it, subsecond underflow would produce incorrect timestamps.

requirements.title: SubtractTime Negative Saturation
requirements.description: The time module shall saturate the result to zero seconds and zero subseconds when subtraction would produce negative time.
requirements.rationale: Saturating to zero prevents negative time values, which have no physical meaning in the library's time model and would cause undefined behavior in unsigned arithmetic.

requirements.title: SubtractTime Negative Error
requirements.description: The time module shall return JUNO_STATUS_INVALID_DATA_ERROR when subtraction would produce negative time.
requirements.rationale: Returning an error status when saturation occurs allows callers to detect and handle the underflow condition rather than silently receiving a clamped result.

requirements.title: Timestamp to Nanoseconds Conversion
requirements.description: The time module shall convert a timestamp to nanoseconds using half-up rounding.
requirements.rationale: Nanosecond conversion enables interfacing with platform APIs and POSIX timespec structures that use nanosecond resolution.

requirements.title: Timestamp to Microseconds Conversion
requirements.description: The time module shall convert a timestamp to microseconds using half-up rounding.
requirements.rationale: Microsecond conversion enables interfacing with platform timers and IO timeout APIs that use microsecond resolution.

requirements.title: Timestamp to Milliseconds Conversion
requirements.description: The time module shall convert a timestamp to milliseconds using half-up rounding.
requirements.rationale: Millisecond conversion enables interfacing with RTOS tick-based APIs and human-readable timing displays.

requirements.title: Nanoseconds to Timestamp Conversion
requirements.description: The time module shall convert nanoseconds to a timestamp with integer seconds and fractional subseconds.
requirements.rationale: Conversion from nanoseconds enables constructing timestamps from platform clock reads and POSIX timespec values.

requirements.title: Microseconds to Timestamp Conversion
requirements.description: The time module shall convert microseconds to a timestamp with integer seconds and fractional subseconds.
requirements.rationale: Conversion from microseconds enables constructing timestamps from timer registers and IO timeout values.

requirements.title: Milliseconds to Timestamp Conversion
requirements.description: The time module shall convert milliseconds to a timestamp with integer seconds and fractional subseconds.
requirements.rationale: Conversion from milliseconds enables constructing timestamps from RTOS tick counts and human-specified delays.

requirements.title: Timestamp to Double Conversion
requirements.description: The time module shall convert a timestamp to a double-precision floating-point seconds value.
requirements.rationale: Double-precision conversion provides a convenient floating-point representation for algorithms that require fractional seconds, such as control loops and telemetry formatting.

requirements.title: Double to Timestamp Conversion
requirements.description: The time module shall convert a non-negative double-precision seconds value to a timestamp.
requirements.rationale: Conversion from double enables constructing timestamps from floating-point time sources and configuration values.

requirements.title: Double to Timestamp Range Rejection
requirements.description: The time module shall return JUNO_STATUS_INVALID_DATA_ERROR for negative or out-of-range double inputs.
requirements.rationale: Rejecting negative and out-of-range double inputs prevents construction of timestamps with undefined or physically meaningless values.

requirements.title: Conversion Overflow Detection
requirements.description: The time module shall return JUNO_STATUS_INVALID_DATA_ERROR when a timestamp-to-integer conversion would overflow.
requirements.rationale: Detecting overflow before it occurs prevents silent truncation or wraparound, which would produce incorrect timing values in downstream consumers.

requirements.title: Null Pointer Rejection
requirements.description: The time module shall return JUNO_STATUS_NULLPTR_ERROR when the time root pointer is NULL.
requirements.rationale: Rejecting a NULL time root pointer prevents null dereference, ensuring runtime memory safety at the module entry point.
